fingerprint: N2's period (12.658h) is determined by the Doodson variable p (lunar perigee advance rate = 0.00464°/h), encoding the moon's anomalistic month (27.55 days) and orbital eccentricity (e ≈ 0.0549) — Keplerian orbital quantities with no flat-disc derivation
globe_value: N2 angular speed σ(N2) = 2τ − s + p, where τ = 14.4921°/h (lunar day), s = 0.5490°/h (moon's sidereal orbital rate = 360°/(27.3217 × 24h)), and p = 0.00464°/h (moon's perigee advance rate = 360°/(8.847 yr × 8766h)). T(N2) = 360°/σ(N2) = 360°/28.4397°/h = 12.658h. The '+p' term is the globe fingerprint: it encodes the rate at which the moon's perigee advances due to solar gravitational perturbation of an elliptical Keplerian orbit. The M2−N2 beat period = 360°/(s − p) = 360°/0.5444°/h = 661.3h = 27.55 days = the anomalistic month (perigee-to-perigee interval). Additionally, the amplitude ratio N2/M2 ≈ 0.19 ≈ 3e encodes the orbital eccentricity e ≈ 0.063 (first-order approximation; actual e = 0.0549).
dome_value: The dome claims N2 'arises from the lunar elliptic circuit' with predicted period 12.6600h. No formula connects dome parameters (disc_radius = 20,015 km, moon_altitude = 2,534 km, firmament_height = 9,086 km) to N2's period. No eccentricity parameter is specified for the 'lunar circuit.' No derivation of the anomalistic month from dome geometry exists. The 'elliptic circuit' is named but never parameterized.
match_precision: N2 period known to 6+ significant figures since Doodson (1921). Dome claims 12.6600h vs observed 12.6580h — 0.016% match, but this is simply quoting the known value.
why_it_matters: N2 is the most geometrically demanding of the five tidal WINs because its very existence requires three Keplerian orbital properties the dome has never specified: (1) The moon must follow an ELLIPTICAL orbit with eccentricity e ≈ 0.0549 — without this, there is no perigee/apogee distinction and no amplitude modulation to generate N2 as a separate constituent from M2. (2) The perigee must ADVANCE at a rate determined by three-body gravitational perturbation (Sun-Earth-Moon) — this is the Doodson variable p = 0.00464°/h, corresponding to an 8.847-year apsidal precession cycle. (3) The anomalistic month (27.55 days) must differ from the sidereal month (27.32 days) by exactly the amount implied by this perigee advance — it is this 0.23-day difference that sets N2's precise frequency offset from M2. None of these quantities can arise from a moon at fixed altitude 2,534 km above a flat disc. On a flat disc, a 'circuit' is a closed path at constant altitude with no concept of perigee, apogee, apsidal precession, or eccentricity. The dome names an 'elliptic circuit' but provides no parameters for the ellipse (semi-major axis, eccentricity, argument of perigee, rate of perigee advance). Without these parameters, the claim is a label with no physics behind it.
suggested_text: N2's period (12.658 hours) is not a standalone number — it is determined by the Doodson decomposition σ(N2) = 2τ − s + p, where the critical variable p (0.00464°/h) is the moon's perigee advance rate. This rate encodes the 8.847-year apsidal precession cycle caused by solar gravitational perturbation of the moon's Keplerian orbit — a three-body gravitational calculation that requires an elliptical orbit with eccentricity e ≈ 0.0549. The dome model names a 'lunar elliptic circuit' but has never specified this eccentricity, never derived the perigee advance rate from dome geometry, and has no code that computes N2 from dome parameters. The M2−N2 beat frequency directly equals the anomalistic month (27.55 days), confirming that N2's frequency offset from M2 is set by the perigee-to-perigee interval — a purely Keplerian quantity.

code_search.found_in: wins.html only — static text: 'The N2 constituent period arises from the lunar elliptic circuit. Predicted period: 12.6600 hours.' Zero code references in inject_ai_layer.py, monitor.py, predictions.js, or formulas.json (133 entries, zero tidal).
code_search.notes: N2 exists ONLY as a static text claim on wins.html. monitor.py tracks M2, K1, and S2 but not N2 or O1. formulas.json has zero tidal formula entries. inject_ai_layer.py has no tidal constants. The dome claims 12.6600h as a 'prediction' but no code computes this from dome parameters — it is the standard NOAA value rounded to 4 significant figures. The phrase 'lunar elliptic circuit' implies an elliptical path but the dome has never parameterized this ellipse (no eccentricity, no semi-major axis, no perigee advance rate). The broader dome framing that 'all major ocean tidal forcing periods are exact consequences of ECM luminary circuit periods — no gravitational perturbation theory required' is directly contradicted by N2, which requires gravitational perturbation theory (specifically solar perturbation causing apsidal precession) to derive its frequency.

globe_variables_required.tau: 14.49205°/h — mean lunar time, requires Earth's rotation period and Moon's orbital period
globe_variables_required.s: 0.54902°/h — Moon's mean sidereal orbital rate, requires Moon orbiting Earth at 384,400 km with T = 27.3217 days (Kepler's third law)
globe_variables_required.p: 0.00464°/h — Moon's perigee advance rate, requires elliptical orbit with eccentricity under solar gravitational perturbation (three-body problem)

doodson_analysis.dome_variables_available: None. The dome specifies moon_altitude = 2,534 km and a 'lunar circuit' but has never published τ, s, or p as dome-derived quantities. The tidal periods on the wins page are stated as known values, not derived from dome geometry.
doodson_analysis.beat_frequency_test: M2 − N2 beat = 360°/(0.54902 − 0.00464) = 360°/0.54438 = 661.3h = 27.55 days = anomalistic month. This confirms N2's frequency offset from M2 is set entirely by the perigee-to-perigee interval — a Keplerian orbital quantity.

eccentricity_fingerprint.implied_eccentricity: e ≈ 0.063 (first-order); actual e = 0.0549 (difference due to higher-order terms)
eccentricity_fingerprint.dome_eccentricity_parameter: ABSENT — the dome model has never specified an orbital eccentricity for its local moon
eccentricity_fingerprint.significance: The amplitude ratio is a second, independent globe fingerprint: not only is N2's period set by Keplerian orbital mechanics (via p), but its amplitude relative to M2 directly encodes the orbital eccentricity. The dome cannot predict N2's amplitude without specifying e.
